title: Data Breach: High Volume Exfiltration to External IP

steps.message: Block the detected external exfiltration IP '45.33.12.98' at the network perimeter (firewall/WAF) to immediately reduce information leakage to a minimum and to withhold sensitive data that can critically undermine an application’s security.

Query the SIEM to identify the internal source host(s) involved in the exfiltration and to perform a forensic examination of application logs, looking for verbose error messages or information disclosure patterns mentioned in the KB (e.g., 'usernames, log entries, and user profile details') that could indicate the initial compromise or data exposure.

params.query: index=* (dest_ip="45.33.12.98" OR (data_exfiltration_alert AND category="data_breach")) | fields _time, host, src_ip, dest_ip, volume, process, user

steps.message: Review all gathered evidence, including the confirmed exfiltrated data volume and type (if captured), and the identified internal source. Authorize the full scope of remediation actions, including potential system isolation and user credential resets, acknowledging the critical impact of confirmed data exfiltration on application security.

Force password resets for any user accounts identified as potentially compromised or whose 'current user’s password' or 'user profile details' may have been involved in or exposed by the data exfiltration incident. Also, revoke all active sessions for these users.

Implement code fixes or patches to address the underlying vulnerability (e.g., SQL injection leading to error-based data extraction, broken access controls) that enabled the information disclosure and subsequent data exfiltration, ensuring the application does not 'publish information that may be of use to an attacker'.

Notify the Security Operations Center (SOC) channel and create a critical incident ticket in the ITSM system detailing the data breach, the exfiltration volume, and the actions taken, emphasizing the need to 'protect by effective access controls and made available only where strictly necessary' going forward.

params.description: Unusually high data exfiltration to external IP 45.33.12.98 detected by DLP. 5GB of data exfiltrated. Initial containment and investigation steps initiated.
